subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Particle Motion
stimulus:
Johanna jogs along a straight path. For 0 (cid:133) t (cid:133) 40, Johanna’s velocity is given by a differentiable function v.
Selected values of v(cid:11)t(cid:12), where t is measured in minutes and v(cid:11)t(cid:12) is measured in meters per minute, are given in
the table above.

parts.prompt: Use the data in the table to estimate the value of v(cid:132)(cid:11)16(cid:12).

scoring_points.description: See College Board scoring guidelines for part (a), point 1

parts.prompt:
Using correct units, explain the meaning of the definite integral (cid:212) v(cid:11)t(cid:12) dt in the context of the problem.
Approximate the value of (cid:212) v(cid:11)t(cid:12) dt using a right Riemann sum with the four subintervals indicated in the
table.

parts.prompt:
Bob is riding his bicycle along the same path. For 0 (cid:133) t (cid:133) 10, Bob’s velocity is modeled by
B(cid:11)t(cid:12) (cid:32) t3 (cid:16) 6t2 (cid:14) 300, where t is measured in minutes and B(cid:11)t(cid:12) is measured in meters per minute.
Find Bob’s acceleration at time t (cid:32) 5.

parts.prompt: Based on the model B from part (c), find Bob’s average velocity during the interval 0 (cid:133) t (cid:133) 10.
